feat(compatibility): BEE-9 add cross-repo matrix + update script + GH Action (#3)

## 📝 Summary

Closes [BEE-9](https://linear.app/beeping/issue/BEE-9) — 📊 Compatibility
matrix cross-repo + automatización.

Builds the cross-repo compatibility tracking infrastructure for the
Beeping Platform: a matrix file with auto-generated and hand-written
sections, a JSON Schema validating its shape, a bash script that
regenerates the matrix from the GitHub API, and a weekly GitHub Action
that runs the script and opens a PR when drift is detected.

## 🎯 Type of change

- [x] ✨ `feat` — new feature (compatibility matrix infrastructure)
- [x] 🤖 `ci` — new GitHub Action workflow

## 📋 Changes

### `compatibility.md` (rewritten)

- Auto-generated section between `<!-- BEGIN AUTO-GENERATED -->` / `<!--
END AUTO-GENERATED -->` markers
- Hand-written sections for rationale, OpenAPI versions, guarantees,
cross-repo contracts, breaking change protocol
- Initial table with all 18 repos at placeholder version `0.0.0`

### `compatibility-schema.json` (new)

- JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) for the matrix structure
- Defines `openapi_versions[]` and `repos[]` shapes
- Reserved for future structural validation in CI

### `scripts/update-compatibility.sh` (new)

- Bash script that fetches `VERSION` from each of the 18 repos via
GitHub API
- Falls back to `0.0.0` if `VERSION` doesn't exist or repo is
inaccessible
- Uses Python (not awk) for the section replacement to avoid BSD awk
multiline string issues
- **Idempotent**: ignores the `Last refreshed` timestamp line when
comparing, so no-op runs don't bump the file
- Tested locally: generates correct table, second run is no-op

### `.github/workflows/update-compatibility.yml` (new)

- Weekly cron (Mondays 04:00 UTC) + manual `workflow_dispatch`
- Runs the script with the built-in `GITHUB_TOKEN`
- Validates markers are present
- Opens a PR via `peter-evans/create-pull-request@v7` if changes
detected
- PR includes a review checklist (downgrades, contracts, ADR, lint,
smoke)
- Permissions scoped to `contents:write` + `pull-requests:write`

## ✅ Definition of Done

- [x] Smoke test passes (50/50 local)
- [x] Markdown lint clean
- [x] Gitleaks clean
- [x] JSON schema valid
- [x] Script tested locally — generates correct table, idempotent on
rerun
- [x] PR linked to a Linear task
- [x] Conventional Commits format used

Fix Windows title bar icon clipped due to non-square source PNG

build/icon.png is 861×859 px (not square). When Electron passes a
non-square nativeImage to BrowserWindow on Windows, the OS crops or
distorts the icon displayed in the title bar and taskbar.

On Windows, resize the loaded nativeImage to 256×256 before handing it
to BrowserWindow so the icon is always square. macOS and Linux are
unaffected (they either use their own icon formats or handle non-square
gracefully via the .icns / AppImage pipelines).

Provider search: second feature port (TDD/BDD) (#2)

* Extend Adapters::Base contract with search_practitioners + find_practitioner (TDD)

Adds the two methods provider_search needs, mirroring the patient
contract from #49:

  search_practitioners(tenant_identifier:, facility_identifier:,
                       name:, specialty:,
                       identifier_system:, identifier_value:)

  find_practitioner(tenant_identifier:, practitioner_identifier:)

Both raise NotImplementedError on the base class so a subclass that
forgets to override one fails loudly. The kwargs use the *_identifier
convention from ADR 0004; the result hash shape on search includes
:practitioner_identifier (opaque, pr_-prefixed), :display_name,
:specialty, :facility_identifier, and :identifiers (FHIR Identifier
shapes for NPI, etc.).

2 new unit tests, total 5 base-contract runs / 13 assertions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Extend MockAdapter with practitioner search/find (TDD)

Mirrors the patient implementation:

- @practitioners hash keyed by tenant_identifier (mirrors @patients)
- seed_practitioner test helper that mints a pr_-prefixed opaque
  identifier and stores the record with display_name, specialty,
  facility_identifier, and an optional identifiers array of FHIR
  Identifier shapes (NPI, DEA, etc.)
- search_practitioners honoring tenant scope, optional facility
  scope, case-insensitive partial name match, case-insensitive
  partial specialty match, and identifier (system + value) match
  via the same match_identifier? helper used for patients
- find_practitioner resolving by opaque identifier within tenant,
  returning nil for unknown identifiers AND for cross-tenant lookups
- public_practitioner_view stripping internal-only keys before
  returning to caller; never exposes a backend-native IEN

12 new unit tests covering: empty start, seed+search, opaque
identifier format, case-insensitive name match, case-insensitive
specialty match, NPI lookup, tenant scoping, facility scoping,
no IEN leak in result keys, find by identifier, find returns nil
for unknown, find returns nil for cross-tenant. All green
(28 runs total in this file, 51 assertions).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add ProviderSearch service (TDD)

Mirrors PatientSearch from #49 — single entry point that reads
tenant and facility scope from Lakeraven::EHR::Current per ADR
0003, raises MissingTenantContextError if tenant is unset, and
delegates to the configured adapter's #search_practitioners.

The service is intentionally a thin coordination layer; all
search semantics live in the adapter so there's only one place
that needs testing for filter behavior. Search params:

  - name (case-insensitive partial match)
  - specialty (case-insensitive partial match)
  - identifier_system / identifier_value (FHIR Identifier pair,
    e.g. NPI via http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/us-npi)

10 unit tests covering: delegation, tenant pass-through (with a
cross-tenant seed to prove the scope), facility pass-through
(set and unset), specialty filter, NPI filter, no-filter
returns-all, MissingTenantContextError on unset tenant, empty
array for no matches, NotConfiguredError on missing adapter.
All green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add provider_search Cucumber feature (BDD)

Second feature port from docs/features.md. Mirrors patient_search
in structure: 10 scenarios stated in user-language exercising the
ProviderSearch service end to end against the in-memory mock adapter.

Scenarios:
- Search by full name
- Search by partial last name (case-insensitive substring)
- Search by specialty (case-insensitive substring on
  Practitioner.qualification text)
- Search by NPI as a FHIR Identifier
  (http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/us-npi system)
- List all practitioners in the current facility (no filter)
- Search returns opaque practitioner identifiers (asserts the
  pr_ prefix and the absence of any backend-native ien key per
  ADR 0002 + 0004)
- Empty result is a valid empty array, not an error
- Search is scoped to the current facility (cross-facility
  isolation per ADR 0003)
- Search across all facilities when facility is unset
- Search fails loud when no tenant context is set
  (MissingTenantContextError per ADR 0003)

The DFN-leak step pattern from patient_search is mirrored here
for IEN — keys are normalized to lowercase strings before the
refute_includes so any casing variant is caught.

All 10 scenarios pass. Total cucumber suite now: 17 scenarios,
134 steps. Total unit suite: 69 runs, 113 assertions.

Patient search: first feature port (TDD/BDD) (#1)

* Add Lakeraven::EHR.configure / .adapter accessor (TDD)

Host applications need a single, conventional way to wire an adapter
into the engine at boot. Patterning after Devise / Doorkeeper /
ActiveStorage:

  Lakeraven::EHR.configure do |config|
    config.adapter = MyAdapter.new
  end

Lakeraven::EHR.adapter raises NotConfiguredError when read before
the host has set one — failure surfaces at boot or first request,
not as a confusing nil downstream.

reset_configuration! exists so the test suite (and Cucumber Before
hooks) can put the engine back to a known state between scenarios
without leaking adapter state across tests.

5 unit tests covering: empty default, configure block, .adapter
accessor, NotConfiguredError when missing, and reset behavior.
All green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Current request store and MissingTenantContextError (TDD)

Lakeraven::EHR::Current is the per-request store ADR 0003 calls for —
holds tenant_identifier and facility_identifier so engine code (and
the fail-loud default scopes that come next) can read them without
threading the values through every method signature.

Built on ActiveSupport::CurrentAttributes for the standard
request-cycle clearing semantics. Adds:

- attribute :tenant_identifier, :facility_identifier
- self.reset! — alias for clear_all so the engine API uses the
  conventional reset! verb (matches the configuration accessor)
- self.with_tenant(value) { ... } — block helper that sets the
  tenant for the duration of the block and restores the previous
  value on exit, including on exception. Background jobs and
  console sessions use this so they don't have to remember to
  reset on the way out.

MissingTenantContextError is the canonical "you forgot to set a
tenant" exception, raised by default scopes per ADR 0003. Defined
here alongside Current so the tenancy machinery is in one place.

8 unit tests covering: defaults, set/read, reset!, with_tenant
nesting, with_tenant exception safety, and the error class
hierarchy. All green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Adapters::Base contract (TDD)

The abstract base class every concrete EHR adapter inherits from.
Declares the two methods patient_search needs:

- search_patients(tenant_identifier:, facility_identifier:, name:,
    identifier_system:, identifier_value:, date_of_birth:)
- find_patient(tenant_identifier:, patient_identifier:)

Both raise NotImplementedError on the base class so a subclass that
forgets to override gets a clear failure rather than a confusing nil.
The kwargs use the *_identifier convention from ADR 0004 throughout.

The contract returns plain hashes — no AR models, no FHIR objects —
because per ADR 0002 the engine never persists what comes back.
Serialization to FHIR happens in the controller layer at request
time and the result is discarded.

Per ADR 0003 every method takes a tenant_identifier so the adapter
can honor tenant scoping at the backend.

3 unit tests covering: search_patients raises on base, find_patient
raises on base, and a subclass implementation works as expected.
All green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Adapters::MockAdapter — in-memory adapter for tests (TDD)

Concrete adapter that holds patients in a Ruby hash keyed by
tenant_identifier. Used by Cucumber and unit tests so the engine
suite stays hermetic — no rpms-rpc, no live broker, no fixtures.

seed_patient is a test helper that takes the seed shape and mints
an opaque pt_-prefixed identifier. The internal record holds
everything; the public_view shape returned to callers strips
internal-only keys and never exposes a backend-native dfn.

search_patients implements the contract honoring tenant scoping
(rows[:tenant_identifier] match), facility scoping when given
(facility_identifier match), and case-insensitive partial name
match (downcased substring).

find_patient resolves by opaque identifier within the tenant scope.
Returns nil for unknown identifiers AND for identifiers that exist
in a different tenant — the cross-tenant test guards against the
common bug where a token leaks across the tenant boundary because
the backend lookup forgot to filter on tenant.

12 unit tests covering: inheritance, empty start, seed+search,
opaque token format, case-insensitive partial name match, tenant
scoping, facility scoping with and without facility filter, no
dfn key in result hashes, find by identifier, find returns nil
for unknown, find returns nil for cross-tenant. All green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add PatientSearch service + Zeitwerk EHR inflection (TDD)

Lakeraven::EHR::PatientSearch is the single entry point for finding
patients. Reads tenant and facility from Lakeraven::EHR::Current per
ADR 0003, raises MissingTenantContextError if tenant is unset, and
delegates to the configured adapter's #search_patients.

The service is a thin coordination layer — no business logic of its
own. Search semantics live in the adapter; the service just enforces
the request-context invariants. This is intentional: it makes the
service uninteresting and the adapter the only place that needs
testing for search behavior.

Zeitwerk inflection: by default Rails autoloading expects
"app/services/lakeraven/ehr/foo.rb" to define Lakeraven::Ehr::Foo,
but the engine module is Lakeraven::EHR (capitalized acronym per
the namespace rename in the bootstrap). Register "ehr" => "EHR"
with every Rails autoloader at engine boot so app/ paths resolve
correctly. Without this, autoloading silently fails to find
PatientSearch and downstream callers see uninitialized constant
errors at request time.

7 unit tests covering: delegation, tenant pass-through, facility
pass-through (set and unset), MissingTenantContextError on unset
tenant, empty array for no matches, NotConfiguredError on missing
adapter. All green.

* Add patient_search Cucumber feature (BDD)

Per the feature-driven port approach in docs/features.md, every
feature in the engine ships with a Cucumber scenario set as its
spec. patient_search is the first feature; this commit lands the
spec, the step glue, and the cucumber harness.

cucumber-rails added as a dev/test dependency. features/support/env.rb
boots the dummy app, requires the engine, brings Minitest::Assertions
into the World (so step defs use assert/refute consistently with the
unit tests rather than introducing a second assertion vocabulary),
and resets engine state between scenarios via a Before hook so one
scenario can't leak adapter or tenant context into the next.

  - Search by partial last name
  - Search returns opaque patient identifiers (asserts the pt_

  - Empty result is a valid empty array, not an error
  - Search is scoped to the current facility (cross-facility
    isolation per ADR 0003)
  - Search across all facilities in the tenant when no facility
    is set
  - Search fails loud when no tenant context is set

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix scaffold files missed by the EHR rename

The Rails plugin generator created four files under
app/{controllers,models,jobs,helpers}/lakeraven/ehr/ that all
declared `module Ehr`. The earlier rename commit (3925530)
updated lib/ and config/ but missed these — they were never
explicitly opened during that pass.

The bug went undetected through ten subsequent commits because:

- Zeitwerk resolves files lazily — nothing in the unit suite or
  the cucumber feature actually loaded ApplicationController,
  ApplicationRecord, ApplicationJob, or ApplicationHelper, so
  the bad constant names never tripped autoloading.
- `rake test` doesn't eager-load by default in test mode, so the
  CI green light didn't catch it either.
- Local development uses lazy loading too, so `rails console`
  worked fine.

The trap reopens the moment anything triggers eager loading:
production boot, `rails app:zeitwerk:check`, or any tool that
walks the autoload tree. The reviewer caught it locally before
the next CI run hit it.

Fix is mechanical — change `module Ehr` to `module EHR` in all
four files. The next commit adds an eager-load regression test
and wires Cucumber + zeitwerk:check into CI so this can never
return undetected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* CI: run cucumber + zeitwerk:check; add eager-load regression test

The previous commit fixed a Zeitwerk inflection trap that the test
suite and CI both missed. The trap was caught manually by a reviewer
running app:zeitwerk:check locally — that's not a sustainable
guarantee. Wire it into CI and the test suite so the next regression
fails loud at PR time.

Three changes:

1. .github/workflows/ci.yml — add a Zeitwerk eager-load step that
   runs `rails app:zeitwerk:check` before the unit tests. If a
   constant doesn't resolve, this fails before the slower test
   steps run, surfacing the cause directly rather than as a
   confusing test failure downstream.

2. .github/workflows/ci.yml — add a Cucumber step. Until now CI
   only ran rake test, so the BDD layer (which is the actual spec
   per docs/features.md) wasn't enforced on PRs. Add it so a
   feature scenario regression also blocks merge.

3. .github/workflows/ci.yml — change pull_request trigger from
   `branches: [main]` (which only fires for PRs targeting main)
   to bare `pull_request` (fires for all PR targets and updates).
   In practice all our PRs target main today, but the narrower
   trigger has bitten people on stacked-PR workflows; leaving it
   open is the safer default.

4. test/lakeraven/ehr/eager_load_test.rb — three regression tests:
   - Force Rails.application.eager_load! and assert it doesn't
     raise. This catches the same class of bug at the unit level
     so contributors don't have to wait for CI to find out.
   - Assert each scaffold constant
     (ApplicationController/Record/Job/Helper) is defined under
     Lakeraven::EHR.
   - Assert no constant is defined under the wrong-cased
     Lakeraven::Ehr namespace. If a future scaffold lands with
     the old casing, this fails immediately.

40 unit tests, 60 assertions, all green. Rubocop clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address Copilot review: 4 fixes + regression coverage

Four findings from Copilot's inline review of PR #1, all real:

1. Current.reset! used clear_all, which resets every
   ActiveSupport::CurrentAttributes subclass in the process — not
   just this engine's. For an embedded engine that's a hostile
   thing to do: it would wipe a host application's own Current
   state every time the engine reset between scenarios. Switched
   to instance.reset, which only resets this class's per-thread
   instance. Added a regression test that defines a sibling
   FakeHostCurrent class, sets a value, calls
   Lakeraven::EHR::Current.reset!, and asserts the host's value
   survives.

2. MockAdapter accepted identifier_system / identifier_value in
   the search_patients signature but silently ignored them. That
   would let identifier-based searches appear to work in tests
   while actually returning name/DOB matches. Implemented proper
   filtering: seed_patient now takes an :identifiers array of
   FHIR-shaped { system:, value: } hashes, and search_patients
   filters rows that match the supplied system and/or value
   (either side may be nil). Added 4 unit tests covering the
   new behavior including a "system alone" lookup and a
   not-found case.

3. test_PatientSearch_passes_the_current_tenant_identifier_to_the_adapter
   only asserted result length, which would have passed even if
   PatientSearch ignored Current.tenant_identifier entirely.
   Strengthened the test to seed an identically-named patient in
   a second tenant and assert it's filtered out — now the test
   actually proves tenant scope is being passed through.

4. The Cucumber DFN-leak step refused :dfn, "dfn", and :DFN but
   missed the "DFN" string variant. Normalized to lowercase
   string keys before checking, so any casing/symbol variant
   the adapter might return is caught by the same single
   refute_includes call.

Result hashes from search_patients now include an :identifiers
key (empty array when none seeded) so the FHIR Identifier
data type can round-trip through the engine without losing
information.

45 unit tests, 69 assertions, all green. 7 cucumber scenarios
still pass. Rubocop clean.

commit.message:
3D liberation phases 6-10: ejection, recycling, passive advection, tidal

Completes the 3D physics liberation. Every force, damping, and advection
function now uses the local orbital frame (r_hat, t_hat, L_hat) instead
of hardcoded XZ-plane geometry.

Phase 6: Ejection kick uses t_hat (toroidal) and L_hat (poloidal) from
         local frame. Jets escape along the particle's actual orbital
         normal, not just along Y.

Phase 7: Boundary recycling respawns along 3D radial direction,
         preserving orbital plane orientation instead of snapping to
         XZ circle.

Phase 8: Passive advection uses Rodrigues rotation around L_hat instead
         of XZ-circle phi advance. Shell drift uses r3d. Velocity
         fix-up writes v_kep along 3D t_hat.

Phase 8b: Active region mask velocity fix-up computes local frame from
          current L on passive→active promotion.

Phase 10: Tidal shear uses r3d instead of r_cyl, removes directional
          L_disk_align penalty (uses |L_disk_align| for coherence).

Shells verified stable over 500 frames with flat-disk init conditions.

commit.message:
fix(security): remove shell=True from generator drift runner (LED-713) (#3)

delimit_security_audit flagged core/generator_drift.py:104 for
subprocess(shell=True) — a real command injection surface, since the
generator_command input flows from the caller's workflow file straight
to a shell.

Fix:
- Parse regen_command with shlex.split and run with shell=False
- Reject shell metacharacters (&, |, ;, >, <, `, $) with a clear error
  message pointing users at script files for chaining/env vars/etc.
- Handle FileNotFoundError explicitly for missing executables
- Handle shlex.split ValueError for unparseable commands
- Reject empty commands

Also adds tests/test_generator_drift.py (17 tests):
- 8 injection-guard tests (chain, pipe, redirect, backtick, $(), ;, empty, unparseable)
- 3 happy-path tests (simple command, drift detection, workspace restore)
- 4 error-handling tests (missing artifact, missing executable, exit code, timeout)
- 2 formatter tests

Verified:
- All 88 prior tests still passing, 105 total (88 existing + 17 new)
- Real agentspec 'pnpm run schema:export' still works (8.1s)
- delimit_security_audit now clean (0 findings)

This fix makes the v1.9.0 release candidate safe to ship. The
previously-rolled-back release bundled the same code with the shell=True
bug.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(server): match fitness results to correct creatures via task_index

Workers return results in arbitrary order (fast creatures finish first),
but evaluate_generation assigned result[i] to task_map[i] — the i-th
result to the i-th task sent. This shuffled fitness across creatures,
destroying selection pressure and making evolution equivalent to random
search.

Fix: EvalTask/EvalResult now carry a task_index that workers echo back.
Results are matched by index, not arrival order.

Also: use NEEDS_EVAL sentinel (NEG_INFINITY) instead of 0.0 to mark
unevaluated creatures, preventing wasteful re-evaluation of creatures
that legitimately scored zero.

commit.message:
refactor(server): in-memory engine with channel-based workers

Replace the DB-as-message-queue architecture with crossbeam channels
and an in-memory evolution engine. Workers receive tasks via channel
(not DB polling), coordinators keep current generation in memory
(not re-reading from DB), and API handlers serve from engine snapshots
(not DB queries). SQLite is relegated to write-behind archival.

Eliminates ~1070 DB ops/5s on the hot path (claim_task, complete_task,
load_gen, poll_pending, API reads) → ~2 writes/generation.

Also defaults num_signal_channels to 0 (paper-faithful).

fix: restore unsafe-inline for Next.js inline scripts

CRITICAL FIX - CSP was too strict and broke the app:
- Next.js requires 'unsafe-inline' for inline hydration scripts
- Without it, pages appear blank (CSP blocks execution)
- Restored 'unsafe-inline' to script-src
- Kept 'unsafe-eval' removed (still improves security)

This is a Next.js limitation - inline scripts are generated
automatically for client-side hydration and cannot be avoided
without using a custom server with nonces.

Security status: GOOD (still better than having no CSP)
- XSS via eval blocked
- All external sources whitelisted
- Only inline scripts allowed (Next.js requirement)
